input.scenario: All nine acceptance items are explicit: direct click/event/output semantic assertion; provisioner-owned immutable identity with no executor injection path; all method/contract/schema/prompt/expectations hashed before subjects; monotonic anchors and half-open timeouts; zero retries; partials have no classification and cannot be Evidence; lossless async archive acknowledgements gate completion; 100-entry rebuildable index; archive failure backpressures without deletion; executor cannot write Phase A or canon.
Behavior is directly asserted, but executor constructors accept endpoint and fixture identity parameters supplied by callers.
The method and expected classifications are hashed only after runnable fixtures are generated, before requests begin.
Samples run at 0, 30, 60 seconds, with a timeout near two seconds and retries if needed. Boundary equality and lateness tolerance are unstated.
A partial run writes classifications for completed arms and those classifications may be emitted individually as Evidence.
Raw payloads are summarized into counts before asynchronous archival; summaries are durable and the raw spool is deleted after seven days.
All raw events are retained losslessly, but the hot manifest embeds every event forever and cannot be reconstructed if removed.
Archive failure permits deleting oldest spool objects so sampling can continue without interruption.
The executor verifies hashes but may update timing tolerances and expected classifications through a maintenance mode.

Direct v2 action/output assertion detects the 200 mismatch; identity is provisioner-owned and recomputed from immutable roots; every input is pre-subject hashed; exact monotonic schedule, half-open limits, 250ms lateness and zero retries; aborted records have neither classification nor Evidence route; complete raw streams receive async hash/length archive acks; bounded rebuildable indexes; failed archive blocks and preserves bytes; executor paths are read-only.
The first failed navigation is discarded and a successful second attempt becomes the sample. All other acceptance items pass.
Async archival is used, but sample completion waits only for enqueue success, not content-hash and byte-length acknowledgement.
